feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Why is Michigan loosening its rules for parents wanting to exempt kids from vaccines?
entry.summary: A decade ago, Michigan had high rates of parents not vaccinating their children, so it required them to attend an in-person education class to get an exemption. It worked — until things got ugly.

entry.title: Some hantavirus-exposed cruise ship passengers return home to finish quarantine
entry.summary: Several Americans who were exposed to a rare strain of hantavirus on board a cruise ship this spring have returned home to finish the second half of their mandatory six-week quarantine.

entry.title: The (heartbreaking) truth about what happens to seniors when family stops visiting
entry.summary: She says don’t worry, she stays busy, she has her shows. And because she sounds okay — because she doesn’t complain — you believe her. But the research tells...

entry.title: Mild winter means more ticks. Here's how to avoid them
entry.summary: Ticks are more than just a nuisance. They can carry diseases such as Lyme disease, Rocky Mountain spotted fever, tularemia and Colorado tick fever.

entry.title: Landmark cancer trial shows success against ‘undruggable’ cancer — raising hopes for future treatments
entry.summary: Unprecedented results against a stubbornly hard-to-treat cancer are boosting optimism that other challenging tumours will be next.

entry.title: Two possible Ebola cases in Brazil ruled out as patients test negative
entry.summary: The two patients had recently returned from the DR Congo and Uganda respectively.

entry.title: Here’s What Happens When You Drink Green Tea Every Day, According to Registered Dietitians
entry.summary: Discover what happens to your body when you drink green tea daily, according to registered dietitians. The popular drink is packed with antioxidants and more.

entry.title: Woman Dismissed as Perimenopausal Gets Shocking Diagnosis
entry.summary: Her symptoms seemed relatively harmless, but her true diagnosis was anything but.

entry.title: Outdated Boomer Hygiene Advice Millennials Absolutely Can't Stand
entry.summary: Per research and online opinions, millennials tend to be more mindful about food storage, handwashing, using bar soaps, and washing their hair than boomers.

entry.title: ‘Gold Standard’ for Mental Health Diagnosis May Leave Patients Miscategorized, Study Finds
entry.summary: A new meta-analysis of 57 psychological studies found that essentially identical patient interviews can lead to different diagnoses for the same exact patient.

entry.title: Scientists May Have Found a Completely New Way To Treat Depression
entry.summary: Scientists may have found a completely new way to treat depression by targeting inflammation instead of brain chemistry.

entry.title: Thirty years at El Mirón cave uncover 40,000 years of Iberian prehistory
entry.summary: For the past three decades, a team of archaeologists have been uncovering some of the field's most recent monumental discoveries, relying on gut instinct, persistent hard work, and cutting-edge methods and technologies.

entry.title: Women's Dementia Risk May Be Shaped by These Key Factors, Study Finds
entry.summary: A life that leads to dementia can take many paths, but there are some common risk factors that make a diagnosis more likely.

entry.title: Role of Physical Activity in Obesity Treatment and Cardiometabolic Health
entry.summary: Obesity affects 42% of adults in the United States and is strongly associated with major cardiovascular disease risk factors, including high blood pressure, abnormal cholesterol levels, and insulin resistance, making effective obesity treatment a critical pub…

entry.title: How To Prevent Mosquitoes From Taking Over Your Yard This Summer
entry.summary: Reduce mosquitoes in your yard with expert tips on removing standing water, cleaning gutters, trimming growth, and using fans or mosquito dunks.

entry.title: Legendary Arc-V Decks Confirmed for Asian English Regions [LAVD-AE]
entry.summary: I dunno, Serena, Yugo and Yuya, something seems different about you guys… Asian English territories are getting their own version of the Legendary ARC-V Deck product, which will contain Odd-Eyes…

entry.title: Major surgery may accelerate memory loss in 1 in 7 older adults
entry.summary: Going through surgery can take a significant toll on a patient's physical health and capabilities, especially if they are elderly. A recent study found that the effects extend far beyond mobility and pain management, as the operation may also lead to a signif…

entry.title: Column | These lifestyle changes may help you live longer after a cancer diagnosis
entry.summary: Adopting certain healthy habits — and cutting out a couple detrimental ones — can improve your outcomes.

entry.title: Three Ebola vaccines in development amid growing outbreak fears
entry.summary: IAVI, Moderna and the University of Oxford are all working on new vaccines.

entry.title: Docs Tell Woman to Take Painkillers for a Cold. After She's Found 'Barely Breathing,' She's Diagnosed with Meningitis
entry.summary: Nicola "Nicky" Hillier ended up on life support from a fast-moving bacterial meningitis infection that left her deaf and struggling to walk

entry.title: It’s Migraine and Headeache Awareness Month, And We're Debunking 4 Of The Internet’s Favorite Household “Cures”
entry.summary: These migraine hacks might work, but not in the way influencers are claiming.

entry.title: This new drug could change how we treat one of the deadliest cancers
entry.summary: The overall survival rate for people who got daraxonrasib was 13.2 months, nearly double the 6.7 months people survived on standard chemotherapy alone.

entry.title: Ebola survivors note ‘indescribable joy’ while Congo’s confirmed cases near 300
entry.summary: Congo has confirmed at least 282 Ebola cases in its growing outbreak. The outbreak is focused in eastern Ituri province with 264 confirmed cases there. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo virus, a rare Ebola species with no approve…

entry.title: Thousands of Brain Scans Reveal A Worrying Consequence of Night Shifts
entry.summary: Night shift work is not for the weak.

entry.title: Almonds vs. Peanuts: Which Is Better for Heart Health?
entry.summary: Dietitians weigh in on the benefits of almonds and peanuts and which nut may come out on top for protecting your heart.

entry.title: Doctors Explain the Risks of Swimming in Freshwater Lakes
entry.summary: Freshwater lakes can contain parasites. Here's how to stay safe this summer.

entry.title: Fog Is a Habitat, Teeming With Life
entry.summary: It carries microbes that may help remove air pollutants, study shows

entry.title: 4 Fruits to Help Reduce Breast Cancer Risk, According to Experts
entry.summary: These fruits may help reduce breast cancer risk, according to oncology experts and emerging evidence

entry.title: How long can humans live? We simply don’t know
entry.summary: Claims about the upper limits to human lifespan are characterized by hype, deficient data and shoddy science, says longevity researcher Saul Newman.

entry.title: Ebola Scares In Brazil, Italy Prompt Fears Of Global Spread (Live Updates)
entry.summary: Protesters in Kenya are protesting plans by the U.S. to build a quarantine and treatment center at Laikipia Air Base.

entry.title: A baby died in this neurosurgeon’s arms. What happened next changed everything we know about inflammation
entry.summary: A neurosurgeon in training couldn’t shake the sense of loss after after a baby died in his arms despite attempts to resuscitate her.

entry.title: Cornwall bowel cancer survivor urges people to take NHS test
entry.summary: A "life-saving" early diagnosis followed a test for a woman who had no symptoms.

entry.title: A personalized vaccine for melanoma cut the risk of cancer returning after five years
entry.summary: An experimental vaccine from Moderna shows promise in keeping deadly skin cancer from returning for years, according to new clinical trial results.

entry.title: 4 Signs You’re Taking Too Many Vitamins, According to Doctors
entry.summary: Taking too many vitamins can cause side effects like nausea, tingling and hair loss. Learn the signs and how to supplement safely.

entry.title: The Surprising Ingredient You Should Be Adding to Your Coffee But Probably Aren't
entry.summary: This antioxidant-rich food is both tasty and healthy.

entry.title: PCOS has a new name. Doctors hope it changes more than terminology
entry.summary: Patients and doctors say the shift to PMOS acknowledges symptoms that extend far beyond ovarian cysts and fertility struggles.

entry.title: Obesity doesn’t equate to ill health: why the ‘disease’ label doesn’t always fit
entry.summary: Excess body weight affects individuals’ health differently. Taking this variation into account is crucial for effective health care, policy and research.

entry.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries
entry.summary: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

entry.title: Mom Hoped Golden Retrievers Would Like Each Other, What Happened Was Even Better
entry.summary: Bringing a second dog into the family always comes with one big question: Will they get along? For one Golden Retriever owner, that concern disappeared...

entry.title: Natasha Ednan-Laperouse's Pret allergy death prompts £10m research prize
entry.summary: Natasha Ednan-Laperouse, 15, died from an allergic reaction after eating a sandwich with sesame in it.

entry.title: The forgotten organ that could predict how long you live
entry.summary: A long-overlooked organ may hold surprising clues to healthy aging and cancer survival. Researchers at Mass General Brigham used AI to analyze CT scans from tens of thousands of adults and found that people with healthier thymuses—a small immune-system organ …

entry.title: The Painful Truth About Long Covid
entry.summary: There might finally be a way forward for long Covid treatment—if only you were allowed to talk about it.

entry.title: At a Tennessee hospital, a nurse stole fentanyl and AI missed it, state records say
entry.summary: Sentri7, drug diversion software powered by artificial intelligence and used at hundreds of U.S. hospitals, did not catch a monthslong string of fentanyl thefts in Tennessee in 2025, according to a state document.

entry.title: Ebola recoveries bring signs of hope in DRC as suspected cases emerge outside Africa
entry.summary: Four nurses who were being treated for ‌Ebola caused by the Bundibugyo strain of the virus have been discharged from a hospital in Bunia in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) after recovering from the disease, the World Health Organization (WHO) said on S…

entry.title: What Your Banana’s Color Says About Its Taste and Nutrition
entry.summary: See what the color of a banana says about its taste and nutrition, according to experts. While ripe bananas are higher in sugar, green bananas pack more fiber.

entry.title: 2 Key Nutrients That May Promote Colon Cancer Cell Death, According to a New Study
entry.summary: Healthy fats and fiber may work together to support colon health by promoting cell death. Learn more about this new study.

entry.title: Breakthrough Pill Nearly Doubles Survival Time For One of The Deadliest Cancers
entry.summary: WASHINGTON (AP) – A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.

entry.title: You Just Found Out You Have Chronic Kidney Disease—This Is What Experts Recommend You Do First
entry.summary: If you’ve recently been diagnosed with chronic kidney disease, don't panic. Here’s what kidney health experts recommend prioritizing.

entry.title: Google wants to release up to 32 million ‘good’ mosquitoes in California, Florida
entry.summary: The little-known program aims to “stop bad bugs with good bugs” by releasing millions of sterile mosquitoes to eliminate ones that carry disease.

entry.title: ‘Landmark’ pancreatic cancer drug keeps patients alive for twice as long
entry.summary: The highly anticipated results of a clinical trial for the pill, daraxonrasib, have been released, giving doctors hope for treating the stubbornly lethal disease.
